heading: Enforcement and penalty
label: 50917 Enforcement and penalty
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)70115(a)49 App.:2617., , §§ 17–19, .Oct. 30, 1984Pub. The words “to any officer or employee of the Department of Transportation” are omitted as surplus because of 49:322(b).In subsection (c)(1), the words “in accordance with ” are omitted for consistency in the revised title and because 5:554 applies to a hearing on the record unless otherwise stated. The words “amount of such” and “modify . . . with or without conditions” are omitted as surplus.Subsection (c)(4) is substituted for 49 App.:2618(b) to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.heading: .—Prohibitions
children.label: a .—Prohibitions
children.text: A person may not violate this chapter, a regulation prescribed under this chapter, or any term of a license issued or transferred under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: enter at a reasonable time a launch site, reentry site, production facility, assembly site of a launch vehicle or reentry vehicle, crew or space flight participant training site, or site at which a payload is integrated with a launch vehicle or reentry vehicle to inspect an object to which this chapter applies or a record or report the Secretary requires be made or kept under this chapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: seize the object, record, or report when there is probable cause to believe the object, record, or report was used, is being used, or likely will be used in violation of this chapter.
The Secretary may delegate a duty or power under this chapter related to enforcement to an officer or employee of another executive agency with the consent of the head of the agency.

After notice and an opportunity for a hearing on the record, a person the Secretary finds to have violated subsection (a) of this section is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of not more than $100,000. A separate violation occurs for each day the violation continues.

children.label: B
children.text: enforce a subpena in an appropriate district court of the United States.
The Secretary shall impose the civil penalty by written notice. The Secretary may compromise or remit a penalty imposed, or that may be imposed, under this section.
The Secretary shall recover a civil penalty not paid after the penalty is final or after a court enters a final judgment for the Secretary.
